Feature: User Statistics Dashboard

Add a comprehensive user statistics feature to the application that provides insights into user activity and engagement.

## Requirements

### 1. Statistics Service
Create a new service that calculates and retrieves user statistics:
- Total number of registered users
- Active users in the last 30 days
- New user registrations in the last 7 days
- User growth rate (percentage change month-over-month)
- Average session duration
- Most active users (top 10)

### 2. API Endpoints
Add the following REST API endpoints:

GET /api/statistics/overview
- Returns overall statistics summary
- Response includes: totalUsers, activeUsers, newUsers, growthRate

GET /api/statistics/users/active
- Returns list of active users with activity metrics
- Supports pagination (limit, offset)
- Supports date range filtering (startDate, endDate)

GET /api/statistics/users/top
- Returns top 10 most active users
- Includes user details and activity count
- Sorted by activity level descending

### 3. Data Models
Create or update models to support statistics:
- UserActivity model to track user actions
- StatisticsCache model to store pre-calculated statistics

### 4. Caching
Implement caching for statistics to improve performance:
- Cache statistics for 1 hour
- Invalidate cache when new users register
- Use in-memory cache or Redis if available

### 5. Testing
Add comprehensive tests:
- Unit tests for statistics calculations
- Integration tests for API endpoints
- Test edge cases (no users, single user, etc.)
- Mock database queries for faster tests

### 6. Documentation
Update documentation:
- Add API endpoint documentation
- Include request/response examples
- Document caching behavior
- Add usage examples

## Technical Details

### File Structure
- Service: src/services/statisticsService.js
- Routes: src/routes/statistics.js
- Models: src/models/UserActivity.js, src/models/StatisticsCache.js
- Tests: tests/unit/statisticsService.test.js, tests/integration/statistics.test.js
- Docs: docs/STATISTICS.md

### Dependencies
May need to add:
- date-fns for date calculations
- node-cache for in-memory caching (if not using Redis)

### Error Handling
- Handle cases where no data is available
- Return appropriate HTTP status codes
- Provide meaningful error messages
- Log errors for debugging

### Performance Considerations
- Use database indexes for date-based queries
- Implement pagination for large result sets
- Cache expensive calculations
- Consider background jobs for statistics updates

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All API endpoints return correct data
2. Statistics calculations are accurate
3. Caching works as expected
4. All tests pass with >80% coverage
5. API documentation is complete
6. Code follows existing project patterns
7. Error handling is comprehensive
8. Performance is acceptable (< 500ms response time)

## Example Usage

```javascript
// Get overview statistics
const response = await fetch('/api/statistics/overview');
const stats = await response.json();
console.log(stats);
// Output: { totalUsers: 1250, activeUsers: 450, newUsers: 23, growthRate: 5.2 }

// Get active users with pagination
const response = await fetch('/api/statistics/users/active?limit=20&offset=0');
const users = await response.json();
console.log(users);
// Output: { users: [...], total: 450, limit: 20, offset: 0 }

// Get top users
const response = await fetch('/api/statistics/users/top');
const topUsers = await response.json();
console.log(topUsers);
// Output: { users: [{ id: 1, name: 'John', activityCount: 523 }, ...] }
```

## Notes

- Follow existing code style and patterns
- Use async/await for asynchronous operations
- Include JSDoc comments for all functions
- Ensure backward compatibility
- Consider future extensibility (e.g., custom date ranges, additional metrics)
